schools.summary: Gill: the land of Canaan, on whose borders they now were, just entering in.
Keil & Delitzsch: come in, possess it, and dwell therein.

themes: when thou art come in unto the land... and possessest it, and dwellest therein
the inheritance present and enjoyed
thanksgiving the response, the Giver owned (Deut. 8:10)

schools.summary: Gill: this oblation differed from the sheaf at barley harvest, the wave loaves at Pentecost, and the cake of the first dough; the firstfruits put in a basket and brought to the place of God's name.

themes: thou shalt take of the first of all the fruit of the earth... in a basket, and shalt go unto the place which the LORD... shall choose
owning God the giver of the harvest
Christ the firstfruits (1 Cor. 15:20)

schools.summary: Gill: he went to the priest whose course it was to minister, or, by the Targum, the high priest, to profess that he had come into the land sworn to the fathers.

themes: I profess this day unto the LORD thy God, that I am come unto the country which the LORD sware unto our fathers
the offering joined to a confession of faith
owning God's faithfulness with the mouth (Heb. 13:15)

schools.summary: Gill: the priest waved it, the waving toward the corners of the earth an acknowledgment of the Lord as proprietor of all; the basket set down before the altar.

themes: the priest shall take the basket out of thine hand, and set it down before the altar
the firstfruits presented to God
owning his lordship, the earth is the LORD's (Ps. 24:1)

schools.summary: Gill: he was to speak with a loud voice before the Lord, the confession recalling the father's low estate and the great nation God made of him.

themes: A Syrian ready to perish was my father... and became there a nation, great, mighty, and populous
humble beginnings magnifying grace
look unto the rock whence ye are hewn (Isa. 51:1)

schools.summary: Gill: they ordered the male children killed by the midwives, and by another edict drowned.
Keil & Delitzsch: the evil entreating, affliction, and hard bondage.

themes: the Egyptians evil entreated us, and afflicted us, and laid upon us hard bondage
the depth of misery recalled
heightening the praise of mercy (Exod. 1:14)

schools.summary: Gill: they cried unto the LORD by reason of their hard bondage; the LORD heard their voice and looked on their affliction, labour, and oppression.

themes: when we cried unto the LORD... the LORD heard our voice, and looked on our affliction
the cry of the oppressed reaching God
this poor man cried, and the LORD heard him (Ps. 34:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the LORD brought them forth out of Egypt after some time.
Clarke: refers to the mighty hand and outstretched arm (Deut. 4:34).

themes: the LORD brought us forth out of Egypt with a mighty hand... and with signs, and with wonders
the redemption wrought by God's power alone
salvation his alone, according to his mercy (Titus 3:5)

schools.summary: Gill: he brought them to this place after forty years' travel through the wilderness; the land flowed with milk and honey.

themes: he hath brought us into this place, and hath given us this land... that floweth with milk and honey
the bringing-in completing the redemption
out, that he might bring in (Deut. 6:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the bringing of the firstfruits of the land.
Wesley: 'it' is the basket of firstfruits.

themes: I have brought the firstfruits of the land, which thou, O LORD, hast given me... and worship before the LORD
offering and worship from remembered grace
what shall I render unto the LORD (Ps. 116:12)

schools.summary: Gill: rejoicing in all the blessings of goodness and mercies of life, which God in his kind providence had favoured them with.
Keil & Delitzsch: rejoicing with house, Levite, and stranger.

themes: thou shalt rejoice in every good thing which the LORD thy God hath given... thou, and the Levite, and the stranger
the firstfruits crowned with thankful joy
worship overflowing in kindness (Phil. 4:4)

schools.summary: Gill, with Maimonides: the tithing is finished at the feast of passover.
Keil & Delitzsch: the tithe of the third year given to the needy.

themes: the third year, which is the year of tithing... that they may eat within thy gates, and be filled
worship bound to charity
the increase held in trust for the poor (James 1:27)

schools.summary: Gill: this declaration was made before the Lord, not in the temple, since the tithe was eaten with the poor in the owner's gates.
Keil & Delitzsch: I have not transgressed nor forgotten thy commandments.

themes: I have brought away the hallowed things out of mine house... I have not transgressed thy commandments
obedience sincere and answerable to God
walking blameless before him (Luke 1:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the hallowed things were not to be eaten in mourning or grief, nor in the defilement of the dead, but with joy; he had hearkened to the voice of the LORD and done all commanded.

themes: I have not eaten thereof in my mourning, neither... for any unclean use, nor... for the dead: but I have hearkened
the holy things kept undefiled
obedience exact in the least (Lev. 22:2)

schools.summary: Gill: this prayer desires that God, from heaven his holy habitation, would look with an eye of love and delight on his people, though unholy in themselves, and bless Israel and the land.

themes: Look down from thy holy habitation, from heaven, and bless thy people Israel, and the land
obedience emboldening the heart to ask blessing
the unholy seeking God's favour in grace (Ps. 80:14)

schools.summary: Gill: these are the words of Moses, referring not only to the laws last mentioned but to all repeated in this book, to be kept with all the heart and soul.

themes: thou shalt therefore keep and do them with all thine heart, and with all thy soul
wholehearted obedience the spirit of all his commands
serving God wholly, not by halves (Deut. 6:5)

schools.summary: Gill: they had affirmed, protested, and most solemnly declared the LORD to be their God, and him only, refusing all other gods, engaging to walk in his ways and keep his statutes.

themes: Thou hast avouched the LORD this day to be thy God, and to walk in his ways
a deliberate, confessed allegiance
the LORD our God will we serve (Josh. 24:24)

schools.summary: Gill: God affirmed and declared them his special people above all on earth, his jewels, his peculiar treasure.
Clarke: the LORD publicly declared them his.

themes: the LORD hath avouched thee this day to be his peculiar people, as he hath promised
the mutual covenant of belonging
a peculiar people, his own treasure (Titus 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: none of the nations had the LORD for their God and King as Israel, nor such laws; these gave them a superiority over all, in praise, name, and honour; they should be a holy people unto the LORD.

themes: to make thee high above all nations... in praise, and in name, and in honour; and that thou mayest be an holy people
the covenant's crown of exaltation and holiness
an holy nation to show his praise (1 Pet. 2:9)
